content:
• Each identity comes with an associated nemesis encounter set. At the start of the game, each player sets aside the cards from their associated nemesis set, out of play; cards from the encounter deck may instruct how to bring the nemesis set into play.
  • A nemesis set is a subset of an identity-specific set and may only be used alongside an identity sharing a set icon.
  • An identity's 'nemesis minion' is the minion belonging to that nemesis set (designated by parenthetical text if multiple minions exist); its 'nemesis side scheme' is the side scheme belonging to that set.

If a non-numerical variable (letter or other symbol) is defined to have a numerical value, treat that variable as the defined value. If undefined (e.g. blanked), treat it as 0.

For costs involving X, the value is defined by card ability or player choice; after which the amount paid may be modified by effects without changing the value of X.
